prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(activate_variable primary_var_1)
(assign_slot_to_variable primary_var_1 resource_slot_1)
(assign_value_candidate primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(confirm_variable_binding primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(reserve_role_a_with_token primary_var_1 component_role_a_1 transient_token_1)
(release_value_candidate primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(activate_variable secondary_var_1)
(assign_slot_to_variable secondary_var_1 resource_slot_2)
(assign_value_candidate secondary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(confirm_variable_binding secondary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(bind_operator_to_variable secondary_var_1 operator_1)
(propose_role_b secondary_var_1 component_role_b_1 domain_value_1)
(confirm_role_b_with_operator secondary_var_1 component_role_b_1 operator_1)
(release_operator_from_variable secondary_var_1 operator_1)
(release_value_candidate secondary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(finalize_role_a_token_assignment primary_var_1 component_role_a_1 domain_value_1 transient_token_1)
(assemble_composite_candidate primary_var_1 secondary_var_1 component_role_a_1 component_role_b_1 composite_candidate_1)
(validate_composite_candidate composite_candidate_1 primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(accept_primary_variable primary_var_1 composite_candidate_1)
(accept_secondary_variable secondary_var_1 composite_candidate_1)
(activate_variable solver_component_1)
(activate_variable solver_component_2)
(assign_slot_to_variable solver_component_1 resource_slot_3)
(release_value_candidate primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate solver_component_1 domain_value_1)
(confirm_variable_binding solver_component_1 domain_value_1)
(install_component_instance solver_component_1 component_instance_1 composite_candidate_1)
(bind_component_instance_phase1 solver_component_1 component_instance_1 composite_candidate_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_feature_to_component solver_component_1 feature_token_1)
(bind_operator_to_variable solver_component_1 operator_1)
(apply_feature_token_to_component_variant1 solver_component_1 feature_token_1 operator_1 component_instance_1 composite_candidate_1)
(release_feature_from_component solver_component_1 feature_token_1)
(release_operator_from_variable solver_component_1 operator_1)
(assign_variant_to_component solver_component_1 variant_token_1)
(apply_variant_to_component_option1 solver_component_1 variant_token_1 component_instance_1 composite_candidate_1)
(release_variant_from_component solver_component_1 variant_token_1)
(accept_component_candidate solver_component_1)
(release_value_candidate solver_component_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(bind_commit_marker primary_var_1 commit_marker_1 domain_value_1)
(finalize_primary_variable_commit primary_var_1 resource_slot_1 commit_marker_1)
(release_value_candidate primary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate secondary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(bind_commit_marker secondary_var_1 commit_marker_1 domain_value_1)
(finalize_secondary_variable_commit secondary_var_1 resource_slot_2 commit_marker_1)
(assign_slot_to_variable solver_component_2 resource_slot_1)
(release_value_candidate secondary_var_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate solver_component_2 domain_value_1)
(confirm_variable_binding solver_component_2 domain_value_1)
(install_component_instance solver_component_2 component_instance_2 composite_candidate_1)
(bind_component_instance_phase1 solver_component_2 component_instance_2 composite_candidate_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_feature_to_component solver_component_2 feature_token_1)
(bind_operator_to_variable solver_component_2 operator_1)
(apply_feature_token_to_component_variant1 solver_component_2 feature_token_1 operator_1 component_instance_2 composite_candidate_1)
(release_feature_from_component solver_component_2 feature_token_1)
(release_operator_from_variable solver_component_2 operator_1)
(assign_variant_to_component solver_component_2 variant_token_1)
(apply_variant_to_component_option1 solver_component_2 variant_token_1 component_instance_2 composite_candidate_1)
(release_variant_from_component solver_component_2 variant_token_1)
(accept_component_candidate solver_component_2)
(release_value_candidate solver_component_2 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate solver_component_1 domain_value_1)
(bind_commit_marker solver_component_1 commit_marker_1 domain_value_1)
(finalize_component_commit solver_component_1 resource_slot_3 commit_marker_1)
(release_value_candidate solver_component_1 domain_value_1)
(assign_value_candidate solver_component_2 domain_value_1)
(bind_commit_marker solver_component_2 commit_marker_1 domain_value_1)
(finalize_component_commit solver_component_2 resource_slot_1 commit_marker_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex constraint satisfaction validation in PDDL. The goal is to commit primary_var_1, secondary_var_1, solver_component_1, and solver_component_2.


## Examining Initial State  
- The problem involves validating a complex constraint satisfaction in PDDL, focusing on committing primary_var_1, secondary_var_1, solver_component_1, and solver_component_2.  
- All variables begin in an inactive state, requiring activation as part of the solution process.

## Examining Initial State  
- Four variables—primary_var_1, secondary_var_1, solver_component_1, and solver_component_2—need activation to proceed.  
- Available slots include resource_slot_1, resource_slot_2, and resource_slot_3, but there are only three slots for four variables.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Each variable requires a unique slot for assignment, suggesting slots must be reused after committing.  
- Committing a variable releases its slot, allowing reassignment, but during the process, timing is critical to avoid shortages.  

## Handling Value Binding  
- Only one domain_value_1 is available, and it becomes unavailable once bound to a variable.  
- Variables likely need to bind to domain_value_1 sequentially, requiring release after binding to allow others to use it.  

## Exploring Commitment Process  
- Committing a variable requires it to be bound to a value, but the commit action doesn’t release the value, keeping it bound.  
- To bind multiple variables, the value must be released after confirming, enabling reuse for subsequent bindings.

## Exploring Role Assignment  
- Primary_var_1 and secondary_var_1 are now ready for assembly, with roles proposed and confirmed for both.  
- The assemble_composite_candidate action constructs the composite, requiring both variables to be ready, roles confirmed, and a composite available.  

## Managing Value Binding  
- Validate_composite_candidate requires primary_var_1 to be bound to domain_value_1, but the value was released earlier, causing an issue.  
- To proceed, primary_var_1 must be rebound to domain_value_1, but secondary_var_1 is currently bound, creating a resource conflict.  

## Investigating Token-Based Approach  
- The token path allows reserving roles without immediate value binding, using transient_token_1 for primary_var_1.  
- Finalizing the role requires binding to domain_value_1 later, avoiding early value conflicts during assembly.

## Extending Role Finalization  
- To finalize primary_var_1's role, release domain_value_1 from primary_var_1 after reserving the role, making it available for secondary_var_1.  
- After secondary_var_1 binds domain_value_1, release it again to rebind to primary_var_1 for finalizing its role with the token.  

## Applying Operator for Secondary Role  
- Use operator_1 for secondary_var_1's role assignment, as the token is already bound to primary_var_1, avoiding conflicts.  
- Confirm secondary_var_1's role with the operator, ensuring it’s ready for assembly alongside primary_var_1.  

## Assembling and Validating Composite  
- Assemble the composite candidate with both variables and their confirmed roles, meeting all preconditions.  
- Validate the composite, confirming primary_var_1 is bound to domain_value_1, completing the candidate checking process.

## Managing Operator Release  
- After confirming the secondary role, release operator_1 from secondary_var_1 to free it for other uses.  
- Bind operator_1 to solver_component_1, enabling feature token application for component variant1.  

## Preparing Component for Evaluation  
- Apply feature_token_1 to solver_component_1 using operator_1, setting it ready_for_feature_binding since role_b isn’t validated yet.  
- Assign variant_token_1 to solver_component_1, then apply option1 for evaluation, ensuring it’s not scored or finalized initially.  

## Finalizing Component Acceptance  
- Accept solver_component_1 as a candidate after evaluation, marking it finalized and candidate_accepted without scoring.  
- This path avoids additional steps like attach_attribute_y, simplifying the process for committing later.  

## Handling Resource Conflicts  
- Check if domain_value_1 is still bound to solver_component_1 after acceptance, as it’s needed for commit but may block solver_component_2.  
- Consider releasing the value from solver_component_1 to free it for binding with solver_component_2, addressing resource slot conflicts.  

## Planning for Multiple Components  
- For solver_component_2, follow a similar process, ensuring value binding and role confirmation before acceptance.  
- Ensure primary_var_1 is accepted into the composite, requiring it to be ready, role-confirmed, and the composite constructed.

## Releasing and Rebinding Values  
- Release domain_value_1 from secondary_var_1 after confirming its role, making it available for other bindings.  
- Rebind domain_value_1 to primary_var_1 to finalize role_a token assignment, ensuring both variables are ready for composite assembly.  

## Assembling Composite Structure  
- Assemble composite_candidate_1 with primary_var_1 and secondary_var_1, ensuring both have confirmed roles and bindings.  
- Validate the composite, confirming primary_var_1 remains bound to domain_value_1, then accept both variables into the composite.  

## Preparing Component Operations  
- Activate solver_component_1 and solver_component_2 early, assigning resource_slot_3 to solver_component_1 for initial setup.  
- Release domain_value_1 from primary_var_1, then rebind it to solver_component_1, confirming the binding for component operations.  

## Binding Component Instances  
- Install component_instance_1 for solver_component_1, binding it to composite_candidate_1 and domain_value_1 in phase1.  
- Assign feature_token_1 to solver_component_1, binding operator_1 to enable feature token application for variant1.

## Releasing Resources
- Release feature_token_1 from solver_component_1 after applying it, ensuring availability for solver_component_2.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
